subject: Anthropology
title: Sapir-Whorf Hypothesis
description: A short lesson to help you apply Sapir-Whorf Hypothesis.

hook.text: The Hopi language has no past tense. Does that mean the Hopi experience time differently? Whorf thought so.

buildup.text: The Sapir-Whorf hypothesis proposes that the language you speak shapes how you perceive and think about the world.

discovery.text: The strong version says language determines thought. The weak version says it merely influences perception.

twist.text: Most linguists reject the strong version. But language does influence color perception and spatial reasoning.

climax.text: Language may not imprison thought, but it certainly nudges it. Different languages highlight different realities.

punchline.text: Your language is a lens, not a cage.

quiz.question: What does the Sapir-Whorf hypothesis propose?
options: Language shapes how you perceive the world
Thought is completely independent of language
